channel.title: Air Purification Texts on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Air Purification Texts on emsenn.net

item.title: Levoit Compact HEPA Purifiers: Core Mini and LV-H132
item.description:
The Problem

How well does a $40 air purifier actually clean air? Compact HEPA purifiers promise hospital-grade filtration in a device the size of a water bottle, but the relationship between filter class, airflow rate, room size, and real-world particle removal is not obvious from a spec sheet. Two numbers that sound similar — “H13 HEPA” and “True HEPA” — may or may not mean the same thing. A CADR rating of 45 CFM sounds low, but low compared to what, and for what room?

item.title: Mid-Range HEPA Air Purifiers: A Comparative Survey
item.description:
The Problem

A buyer shopping for a portable air purifier in the $80-200 range faces five or six well-reviewed models that all claim “True HEPA” filtration, all cite room coverage of 200+ square feet, and all show four- or five-star ratings. The spec sheets look similar enough that differences blur into marketing noise. But the performance gap between the cheapest and most expensive models in this range is not marginal — it is roughly a 2x difference in clean air delivery, which means one unit provides effective filtration in a 150 sq ft bedroom while another handles a 300 sq ft living room.
